name: Google Cloud Platform
description: Google Cloud Platform provides 100+ services across 35+ regions, with particular strengths in data analytics, machine learning, and Kubernetes (GKE). Google pioneered BeyondCorp zero-trust architecture and builds security into its infrastructure with custom-designed hardware (Titan security chips), encrypted-by-default data at rest and in transit, and a global private network.

securityModel.name: Google Cloud Shared Responsibility Model
securityModel.description: Google is responsible for securing the underlying infrastructure including hardware, firmware, networking, and the software stack that runs Google Cloud services. Customers are responsible for securing their workloads including data, identity, application logic, and configuration of cloud services. Google refers to this as shared fate, actively investing in customer security success.

layers.scope: Hardware (Titan chips), boot stack, inter-service communication, storage encryption, network infrastructure, DDoS protection
Data classification and protection, identity and access management, application security, network configuration, OS patching (for GCE), audit logging configuration

frameworks.name: CIS Google Cloud Platform Foundation Benchmark
frameworks.description: Consensus-based security configuration guidelines for GCP projects and organizations. Covers IAM, logging and monitoring, networking, VMs, storage, Cloud SQL, BigQuery, and KMS. Published by the Center for Internet Security.

frameworks.name: Google Cloud Security Foundations Blueprint
frameworks.description: Google-authored prescriptive architecture for establishing a secure GCP environment. Covers organization structure, authentication, resource hierarchy, networking (hub-and-spoke or Shared VPC), logging, detective controls, and security operations. Deployable via Terraform Foundation Toolkit.

frameworks.name: Google Cloud Architecture Framework - Security Pillar
frameworks.description: Best practices and design recommendations for building secure workloads on GCP. Covers identity, data protection, network security, compute security, logging, detection, and incident response.

malaysiaContext.notes: GCP does not have a region in Malaysia. The nearest region is asia-southeast1 (Singapore). A second Southeast Asia region exists in Jakarta (asia-southeast2). For Malaysian PDPA 2010 data residency requirements, evaluate Singapore or Jakarta options. GCP Assured Workloads can help meet regulatory requirements for specific jurisdictions.
